items.label: Blue Monster Returns:
items.body: The PGA Tour returns to Trump National Doral’s Blue Monster for the Cadillac Championship, bringing a limited-field Signature Event to one of golf’s most demanding venues.

items.label: Signature Event Stakes:
items.body: The event is a 72-player, no-cut Signature Event scheduled for April 30 to May 3, with a $20 million purse and a full four rounds guaranteed for every player.

items.label: Scheffler Headlines Field:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler is the headline name in the latest field and remains the clear center of the betting market.

items.label: Koepka First Alternate:
items.body: Brooks Koepka is listed as the first alternate in the latest field update, keeping the final entry picture worth monitoring until the week begins.

items.label: Field Still Finalizing:
items.body: The PGA Tour’s field notes indicate the event’s entrant list is being finalized through the latest qualification and alternates process.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+450 to +550):
items.body: The betting favorite and the most complete player in the field, with elite tee-to-green form built for a demanding, penalty-heavy course.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+900 to +1100):
items.body: A premium power-and-shotmaking contender whose length and long-iron play can create separation at Doral.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1400 to +1600):
items.body: A steady championship performer whose balanced game fits a no-cut test with little margin for error.

items.label: Collin Morikawa (+1600 to +1800):
items.body: One of the best iron players in golf, and the type of precision player who can thrive if the Blue Monster plays firm.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1800 to +2200):
items.body: A high-ceiling young star with enough power and ball-striking upside to contend on a long, exacting track.

items.label: Justin Thomas (+2200 to +2500):
items.body: A dangerous elite-field player whose creativity and wedge play make him a live threat if the putter heats up.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2500 to +3000):
items.body: A veteran ball striker whose approach game can keep him in contention over four demanding rounds.

items.label: Brooks Koepka (+2500 to +3500 if he gets in):
items.body: The current first alternate, but still a major-level threat if a late withdrawal opens a spot.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Trump National Doral’s Blue Monster Course in Miami, Florida.

items.label: Return to Doral:
items.body: This marks the Blue Monster’s return to the PGA Tour schedule after a long absence, making the event a notable reset for a classic championship layout.

items.label: Course Identity:
items.body: The Blue Monster is known for water hazards, long sight lines, and pressure-packed finishing holes that can quickly reshape the leaderboard.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Trump National Doral - Blue Monster Course, Miami, Florida.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: April 30 through May 3, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $20 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: Signature Event, 72 players, no cut.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: PGA Tour coverage is expected under the standard Signature Event media package.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: The tournament is scheduled for April 30 to May 3, 2026.
